meta.title: Emotional Design Principles for SOTY Event Horizon
meta.author: Cleanlystudio
meta.description: Comprehensive emotional design database covering Norman's levels, awe triggers, frisson science, and flow state engineering

don_norman_three_levels.source: Emotional Design: Why We Love (or Hate) Everyday Things — Don Norman, 2004

visceral.description: The immediate, gut-level reaction before any conscious thought. Primal, automatic, universal across cultures.

event_horizon_application.first_impression: The loading screen and first frame must be viscerally beautiful. Pure black transitioning to the first stars.
event_horizon_application.visual_quality: 60fps, anti-aliased particles, smooth gradients — any visual imperfection breaks visceral appeal
event_horizon_application.color_impact: Deep blacks, vivid accretion disk colors, crisp cyan HUD — high contrast on dark = instant visual impact
event_horizon_application.sound: First audio impression: low sub-bass drone that vibrates the chest. Immediate physical reaction.

design_checklist: Does the first frame take your breath away?
Is the visual quality flawless (no aliasing, banding, jank)?
Do the colors create an immediate emotional response?
Does the audio enhance or detract from the visual impact?
Would a person who speaks no language still find it beautiful?

behavioral.description: The experience of use — usability, interaction feedback, flow. Not how it looks, but how it feels to use.
components: Clear feedback (hover states, press states, progress indicators)
Error prevention (can't scroll past boundaries accidentally)
Recovery (can scroll back, can navigate to any chapter)
negative_experiences: Scroll lag or jank (breaks the illusion of control)
Unclear navigation (where am I? how far along?)
No way to skip or navigate (trapped feeling)

event_horizon_application.scroll_response: Scroll input must produce visual change within 1 frame (16ms). Any lag breaks the sense of control.
event_horizon_application.progress_feedback: Chapter indicators, scroll progress bar, or visual cues showing position in the narrative
event_horizon_application.navigation: Option to jump between chapters. Keyboard/gamepad support. Accessibility considerations.
event_horizon_application.interaction_feedback: Every clickable element has hover, active, and focus states. Sound feedback on key interactions.

design_checklist: Does scroll feel smooth and responsive?
Can the user tell where they are in the experience?
Can they navigate forward AND backward easily?
Do interactive elements provide clear feedback?
Is the experience accessible (keyboard, reduced motion)?

reflective.description: The conscious, post-experience reflection. Meaning, story, identity, pride. What stays with you after the experience ends.
reflective.processing_time: Minutes to years — the lasting impression
components: Personal connection (did it make me think/feel?)
Identity (do I feel smarter/cooler for having experienced this?)
Share-worthiness (would I tell someone about this?)
Pride of discovery (I found something amazing)

event_horizon_application.narrative: The story of a black hole must be compelling enough to remember. Not just visuals — ideas that resonate.
event_horizon_application.meaning: Each chapter should leave the viewer with a new understanding or feeling about the cosmos and their place in it
event_horizon_application.shareability: The experience should be so remarkable that viewers want to share the URL. This is how SOTY nominees go viral.
event_horizon_application.identity: After experiencing Event Horizon, the viewer should feel they've encountered something intellectually and emotionally significant
event_horizon_application.memory_anchors: Specific moments designed to be unforgettable: the first view of the black hole, the crossing moment, the final revelation

design_checklist: Does the experience leave a lasting impression?
Would someone talk about it the next day?
Does it make the viewer feel something meaningful?
Is there a narrative arc with a satisfying resolution?
Does it reward revisiting (noticing new details)?

emotional_triggers_for_awe.description: Awe is defined as the feeling of being in the presence of something vast that transcends current understanding (Keltner & Haidt, 2003)

components_of_awe.perceived_vastness: Physical (Grand Canyon), conceptual (infinity), temporal (deep time), social (collective humanity)
components_of_awe.need_for_accommodation: The experience challenges existing mental frameworks — forces the viewer to update their understanding

scale.description: The sense of something overwhelmingly large or small — vastness that dwarfs the viewer
scale.psychological_mechanism: Activates the 'small self' — reduces ego, increases humility, promotes prosocial behavior
implementation: The black hole should feel MASSIVE — several orders of magnitude larger than anything on screen
Zoom from galactic scale to human scale to quantum scale — show the range
Compare the black hole to familiar objects: 'This black hole is 20 million times the mass of our Sun'
Use the viewport itself as a sense of scale — the black hole should extend beyond the screen edges
scale.measurable_effect: Studies show awe from vastness reduces inflammatory cytokines and increases feelings of social connection

mystery.description: The unknown, the unrevealed, the sense that there is more beyond comprehension
mystery.psychological_mechanism: Triggers curiosity and approach behavior. The brain seeks resolution of uncertainty.
implementation: What's beyond the event horizon? Never fully answer — preserve the mystery
Reveal information gradually, not all at once. Each scroll reveals more but also raises questions
Use visual obscurity — the black hole's interior is literally invisible. Lean into this.
Sound design: low-frequency rumbles suggest something massive and unknowable

danger.description: Proximity to something powerful or destructive — but from a safe distance
danger.psychological_mechanism: Produces awe when the threat is perceived as non-immediate (aesthetic distance). Fight-or-flight arousal channeled into wonder.
implementation: Show what happens to matter near the black hole — spaghettification, destruction
The viewer is 'approaching' something fatal — create tension through proximity
Tidal forces visualization: show how the body would be torn apart
The point of no return (event horizon) is a threshold of danger — build anticipation before crossing

beauty.description: Unexpected visual moments of pure aesthetic pleasure
beauty.psychological_mechanism: Activates reward centers (nucleus accumbens). Beauty in unexpected contexts amplifies the effect.
implementation: The accretion disk is genuinely beautiful — make it mesmerizing, not just accurate
Gravitational lensing creates surreal visual distortions — make these moments of unexpected beauty
Star fields should be breathtaking in their density and depth
The contrast of destruction (black hole) with beauty (accretion disk, lensing) creates emotional tension

sound.description: Audio that enhances and amplifies the visual emotional experience
sound.psychological_mechanism: Audio processes faster than vision. Sound can prime emotional response before visual registers.
implementation: Sub-bass (20-60Hz) for the black hole's presence — felt in the chest more than heard
Crescendos synchronized with visual reveals — audio peaks 200ms before visual peak for anticipation
Silence as a tool — complete audio void during the event horizon crossing creates powerful contrast
Shepard tone (infinitely descending pitch) for the approach — creates perpetual falling sensation

time.description: Perception of time distortion — deep time, time dilation, temporal vastness
time.psychological_mechanism: Disrupting normal time perception creates feelings of transcendence and timelessness
implementation: Time dilation near the black hole — show clocks slowing, show time readouts decelerating
Cosmic time scales — 'This black hole has existed for 10 billion years'
Real-time vs cosmic time contrast: 'In the time it took you to read this, the black hole consumed...'
Scroll speed can map to time dilation — slower scroll = more time passing in the narrative

research_findings.finding: Awe experiences reduce activity in the default mode network (DMN) — the brain's 'self' network
research_findings.implication: People literally think less about themselves during awe — they are fully present

research_findings.finding: Awe dilates subjective time perception — moments feel longer
research_findings.implication: Design peak moments to be lingered on, not rushed through

research_findings.finding: Awe increases prosocial behavior, generosity, and feeling connected to others
research_findings.implication: A shared awe experience (like showing someone the site) creates social bonding

research_findings.finding: Awe is most reliably triggered by nature (43%), art (24%), and music (18%)
research_findings.implication: Combine natural (cosmic) visuals with artistic composition and powerful audio

research_findings.finding: Brief exposure (2-3 minutes) to awe-inducing stimuli produces measurable psychological effects
research_findings.implication: Even a short visit to Event Horizon should produce awe if designed correctly

frisson_response.description: Frisson (aesthetic chills/goosebumps) — involuntary physiological response to emotionally powerful stimuli. Piloerection, tingling, shiver down the spine.
frisson_response.prevalence: 55-86% of people experience frisson, depending on the study and trigger type
frisson_response.personality_correlation: Strongly correlated with 'openness to experience' (Big Five personality trait)

musical_triggers.description: Music is the most reliable frisson trigger (reported in 90% of frisson-capable individuals)

specific_triggers.description: Gradual volume increase reaching a peak
Modulation to a new key, especially upward
specific_triggers.example: Moving from minor to major key

specific_triggers.reason: Human voice is the most emotionally resonant instrument

specific_triggers.description: Sub-bass building to high frequency brilliance
specific_triggers.reason: Activates progressively more auditory nerve fibers
Brain continues processing the absent stimulus — ghosting effect

musical_triggers.optimal_pattern: Build tension (30-60 seconds) → false resolution → rebuild → true climax → brief silence → resolution
event_horizon_audio_arc: Opening: sparse, occasional deep tones — curiosity
Approach: growing drone, increasing overtones — anticipation
First view: sub-bass drop + high string entrance — awe
Education: rhythmic pulse, data sonification — engagement
Event horizon: Shepard tone descent, increasing intensity — dread
Crossing: MAXIMUM INTENSITY → sudden total silence — frisson peak
Beyond: single pure tone emerging from silence — resolution + possible second frisson

visual_triggers.description: Visual stimuli that trigger frisson, especially in combination with audio

specific_triggers.description: Sudden realization of the true size of something
specific_triggers.implementation: Zoom out from detail to show the full black hole for the first time
The perfect accretion disk distorts as we approach — reality warping

specific_triggers.description: Bright element appearing in deep darkness
specific_triggers.implementation: First star, first accretion glow, singularity flash

specific_triggers.description: Many elements converging to a single point
specific_triggers.implementation: All particles streaming toward the singularity
Warm accretion disk transitioning to cold blue at the event horizon

specific_triggers.description: The viewer suddenly understands something profound
specific_triggers.example: Realizing that time stops at the event horizon — loved ones age while you freeze

specific_triggers.description: Understanding one's smallness in the cosmic context
specific_triggers.example: 'You are seeing light that has traveled 55 million years to reach your screen'
The moment of crossing the event horizon — there is no going back

specific_triggers.description: Feeling connected to the cosmos or all of humanity
specific_triggers.example: 'Every atom in your body was forged in a star that exploded billions of years ago'

maximizing_frisson_probability.combination: Combine audio + visual + narrative triggers simultaneously for maximum frisson probability
maximizing_frisson_probability.timing: The viewer should be emotionally primed for at least 30 seconds before the frisson trigger
maximizing_frisson_probability.contrast: Frisson requires contrast — build from quiet/dark/slow to intense/bright/fast (or vice versa)
maximizing_frisson_probability.personal_relevance: The more personally meaningful the content, the higher the frisson probability
maximizing_frisson_probability.repeated_exposure: Frisson diminishes with repetition — design multiple frisson points, not just one

flow_state_design.source: Mihaly Csikszentmihalyi — Flow: The Psychology of Optimal Experience (1990)
flow_state_design.description: Flow state is complete immersion in an activity — loss of self-consciousness, distorted time perception, intrinsic reward

challenge_skill_balance.description: The difficulty must match the user's ability. Too easy = boredom. Too hard = anxiety.
challenge_skill_balance.web_application: The narrative must be comprehensible but not trivial. Scientific concepts explained clearly enough to follow but complex enough to engage.
challenge_skill_balance.event_horizon: Balance scientific accuracy with accessibility. Don't dumb down, but don't require a physics PhD. Visual metaphors bridge the gap.

clear_goals.description: The user must understand what they're working toward at each moment.
clear_goals.web_application: Chapter structure provides clear goals — the user knows they're progressing through a narrative with a destination.
clear_goals.event_horizon: Each chapter has a clear purpose: learn about the black hole's anatomy, approach it, cross the event horizon. The goal is always the next discovery.

immediate_feedback.description: Every action produces a clear, immediate response.
immediate_feedback.web_application: Scroll produces immediate visual change. Interactive elements respond instantly.
immediate_feedback.event_horizon: Scroll = visual change within 16ms. No dead zones where scrolling produces no visual response. Every scroll pixel should produce visible change.

concentration_and_focus.description: The activity must demand enough attention to exclude distractions.
concentration_and_focus.web_application: The visual richness and narrative depth should fully engage both visual and cognitive processing.
concentration_and_focus.event_horizon: Full-screen immersive experience with audio removes external context. The black hole demands attention through visual magnetism.

loss_of_self_consciousness.description: The user stops thinking about themselves and becomes one with the experience.
loss_of_self_consciousness.web_application: No UI elements that break immersion (intrusive cookie banners, social media buttons, newsletter popups).
loss_of_self_consciousness.event_horizon: Minimal UI chrome. The viewer should forget they're on a website. No breaking of the fourth wall.

transformation_of_time.description: Time perception distorts — hours feel like minutes (or vice versa).
transformation_of_time.web_application: The experience should feel timeless. No visible clocks, no countdown timers (unless narrative purpose).
transformation_of_time.event_horizon: Time dilation is literally part of the story — use this thematic element to reinforce the experiential time distortion.

intrinsic_reward.description: The activity is rewarding in itself, not for external reasons.
intrinsic_reward.web_application: The viewer scrolls because they WANT to see what's next, not because they're told to.
intrinsic_reward.event_horizon: Every chapter should end with a visual or narrative reward that makes the viewer eager for the next chapter.

flow_channel.description: The optimal experience zone between anxiety (too challenging) and boredom (too easy)

map.anxiety_zone: Too much information, too fast. Complex visualizations without explanation. Overwhelming audio.
map.flow_channel: Information revealed at the pace the viewer can absorb. Visual complexity matches comprehension. Audio supports without overwhelming.
map.boredom_zone: Static screens. Long text blocks with no visual change. Repetitive patterns. Obvious information.

pacing_for_flow: Alternate between high-intensity visual moments and contemplative text sections
Never maintain the same intensity for more than 30-60 seconds
After a visual peak, allow a 'breathing' moment before the next buildup
Vary the pace: fast approach sequences → slow data reveals → medium narrative → fast crossing

emotional_arc_for_event_horizon.description: The deliberate emotional journey the viewer should experience from start to finish
emotional_arc_for_event_horizon.overall_arc: Curiosity → Wonder → Knowledge → Tension → Awe → Terror → Transcendence → Reflection

techniques: Dark, near-empty screen with subtle star field — 'what am I looking at?'
Single, quiet audio tone — expectation
Minimal text — let the darkness speak
Slow reveal of first light elements
prologue.audio: Near-silence with occasional deep reverberant tones

techniques: Stars begin to stream past — sense of movement and direction
First glimpse of the black hole silhouette against the star field
Text introduces the concept — 'You are approaching the most powerful force in the universe'
Audio builds slowly — adding layers of drone and harmonics
the_approach.color_temperature: Cool transitioning to warm at edges
the_approach.audio: Building drone with increasing overtone complexity

techniques: Detailed visualization of accretion disk, photon sphere, ergosphere
Data appears: mass, radius, temperature — the HUD activates
Each component explained through visual and text
Interactive moments — hover/click for additional data layers
anatomy.color_temperature: Mixed — warm accretion with cool data overlays

techniques: Visual distortion increases — gravitational lensing becomes extreme
Time dilation effects — clock elements slowing
The point of no return approaches — text becomes more urgent
Audio: Shepard tone begins (infinite descent illusion)
Color shifts toward more extreme contrasts
event_horizon.color_temperature: Extreme contrast — hot accretion vs cold void
event_horizon.audio: Shepard tone, increasing sub-bass, heartbeat-like rhythm

techniques: MAXIMUM visual intensity — everything distorts
Spaghettification visualization
Reality appears to break down — visual glitches, chromatic aberration
Text becomes fragmented, distorted
Audio: PEAK INTENSITY → sudden TOTAL SILENCE at the exact crossing moment
Screen goes to absolute black (pure #000000) for 1-2 seconds
the_crossing.audio: Crescendo to maximum → total silence (frisson trigger)

techniques: New visual state emerges from the void — inverted colors? new geometry?
Physics breaks down — visual language changes completely
Time and space lose meaning — the visualization becomes abstract
Text: philosophical, poetic — 'What lies beyond cannot be known'
Audio: single pure tone, slowly growing, ethereal
beyond.audio: Pure tone emerging from silence, slowly gaining harmonics

techniques: Visual calms to final state — resolution
Return to recognizable visual language — stars, space
Final text: reflective, connecting the cosmic to the personal
Audio: resolves to major key, warm, complete
Credits, sharing options, call to explore more
epilogue.audio: Resolving harmony, fading to contemplative silence

peak_emotional_moments.description: Specific moments engineered for maximum emotional impact

moments.name: First sight of the black hole
moments.technique: After building anticipation with approaching stars, the black hole silhouette appears — a VOID in the star field. Not a visual addition, but a visual ABSENCE.
moments.audio_sync: Sub-bass drone enters 500ms before visual reveal
moments.duration: 2-3 seconds of pure visual impact before any text

moments.name: Scale realization
moments.technique: Show the Earth next to the black hole for scale comparison. The Earth is a pixel. The black hole is the viewport.
moments.audio_sync: Volume dip (inhale moment) before the comparison appears

moments.name: Time dilation visualization
moments.technique: Side-by-side: a clock near the black hole vs one far away. Near clock crawls. Far clock races. Implication: your loved ones age while you freeze.
moments.audio_sync: Clock ticking that decelerates to near-silence

moments.name: The crossing moment
moments.technique: TOTAL sensory peak followed by TOTAL sensory void. Maximum visual intensity + maximum audio intensity → cut to pure black + total silence. 1.5 seconds of nothing.
moments.audio_sync: Crescendo peaks → hard cut to silence (not fade — CUT)

moments.name: Emergence from the void
moments.technique: A single point of light appears in the absolute darkness. Grows slowly. New sounds emerge. The viewer has 'survived' the black hole.
moments.audio_sync: Single pure sine tone at ~440Hz (concert A — the universal reference pitch)
moments.duration: Light appears over 2-3 seconds, grows over 5 seconds

silence_as_emotional_tool.description: The deliberate use of silence (audio and visual void) as an emotional device
principles: Silence after intensity creates the strongest emotional contrast — the brain continues processing the now-absent stimulus
2-3 seconds of silence is the optimal duration — shorter feels like a glitch, longer feels like an error
Silence must be EARNED — it only works after building intensity. Silence at the start is just empty.
Total silence + total darkness = the void. This is the Event Horizon's most powerful moment.
The return from silence should be gentle — a whisper, a single tone, a single pixel of light

implementation.audio: audioContext.suspend() for TRUE silence, not just volume 0 (which may have noise floor)
implementation.visual: Render pure #000000 — no particles, no stars, no HUD. Absolute nothing.
implementation.timing: 1.5-2.5 seconds of total void. Shorter if the audience might think it's broken.

silence_as_emotional_tool.psychological_effect: Triggers the brain's 'gap detection' mechanism. The absence of expected stimuli creates heightened awareness and emotional sensitivity. The next stimulus after silence is perceived as more significant.

catharsis.description: Aristotle's concept of emotional purging through art — the release of accumulated tension at the conclusion
components: Tension must be built systematically throughout the experience
The release must feel proportional to the buildup — a gentle buildup requires a gentle release; an intense buildup demands an intense release
After catharsis, the viewer should feel changed — calmer, wiser, more connected
The resolution should return to a modified version of the beginning state (transformed, not just reset)

event_horizon_catharsis.buildup: 60-80% of the experience builds tension through approach, education, and event horizon crossing
event_horizon_catharsis.release: The void moment (silence) is the cathartic break. The emergence after is the resolution.
event_horizon_catharsis.transformed_return: The final star field should feel DIFFERENT from the opening — the viewer has been through the black hole. Same stars, but the viewer has changed.

micro_emotional_design.description: Small emotional design details that accumulate into overall experience quality

details.emotional_effect: A custom cursor that responds to the scene (grows near interactive elements, stretches toward the black hole) makes the user's presence feel integrated into the experience
Fast scrolling produces a 'warp speed' effect (star streaks). Slow scrolling allows contemplation. The experience adapts to the user's pace.
Subtle audio feedback on interactive elements (not annoying clicks — gentle tones) makes the interface feel alive
Subtle pulsing of ambient light (1-2% opacity variation on a 4-6 second cycle) creates a subconscious sense that the scene is alive
Particles that subtly react to cursor position (gravitational attraction or repulsion) create a sense of agency and presence in the scene
Instead of a generic loader, show the loading process as 'calibrating instruments' or 'establishing connection' — the loading IS part of the experience

sources.author: Menninghaus et al.

sources.title: Getting Aesthetic Chills from Music: The Connection between Openness to Experience and Frisson
sources.author: Mitchell Colver & Amani El-Alayli

sources.title: Awe, the Small Self, and Prosocial Behavior
sources.author: Piff, Dietze, Feinberg, Stancato, Keltner

sources.title: 100 Things Every Designer Needs to Know About People
sources.author: Susan Weinschenk
